name: France MFI rate — new loans to households for house purchase
notes: §2.A A6 resolved 2026-07-09 (credentialed BDF_API_KEY probe). Banque de France's Webstat migrated from the legacy IBM API Connect stack to an Opendatasoft Explore v2.1 instance where every observation lives in ONE flat store dataset `observations` keyed by `series_key` (the per-series `mir-*` catalogue datasets are empty stubs, has_records=False). Fetch form is therefore `observations|series_key='<DOT_KEY>'`, not one-dataset-per-series. The legacy ECB-harmonised dot-key M.FR.B.A2C.A.R.A.2250.EUR.N does NOT exist in the BdF store; the BdF-published headline is MIR1.M.FR.B.A22.A.R.A.2250U6.EUR.N = "New loans for house purchase, households, annual interest rate" (A22 aggregate over all PFIT, U6 aggregation). Verified live via adapter 2026-07-09: 281 obs 2003-01 -> 2026-05 = 3.10%.

name: France MFI rate — new loans to non-financial corporations
notes: §2.A A6 resolved 2026-07-09 (credentialed BDF_API_KEY probe). Same Webstat Opendatasoft flat-store model as FRA_LOAN_RATE_HOUSE — all obs in the single `observations` dataset keyed by `series_key`; fetch form `observations|series_key='<DOT_KEY>'`. The legacy dot-key M.FR.B.A2A.A.R.A.2240.EUR.N does NOT exist in the BdF store; the BdF-published headline is MIR1.M.FR.B.A20.A.R.A.2240U6.EUR.N = "New loan, non-financial corporations, annual interest rate" (A20 aggregate, U6). Verified live via adapter 2026-07-09: 281 obs 2003-01 -> 2026-05 = 3.54%.
